_provenance: Hand-derived from the Phase-3b acceptance-gate design (01-acceptance-gate-design.md) and PHASE3_DECISIONS.md. All scenario inputs are synthetic — no producer-computed rates or percents are pinned. Verdicts are drawn from the defined enum {ADOPT_CANDIDATE, KEEP_INCUMBENT, REJECT_VETO_FAILED}. Panel scores are asserted as None/non-None, never as specific floats, because the gate spec says panel_score is None whenever any veto fails (the one-directional-brake property). Weight constants are named (not literals) per no-magic-numbers rule.

all_vetoes_pass_oos_superior_panel_adopt._description: Happy path: all three vetoes pass (BHY gate cleared, NN1 spec frozen, purge integrity intact), OOS is strictly superior to both baselines, panel prefers the candidate over incumbent. Expected decision: ADOPT_CANDIDATE.
all_vetoes_pass_oos_superior_panel_adopt._invariant: panel_score must be a float (not None) when all vetoes pass — the one-directional brake must NOT suppress it.

bhy_veto_fails_panel_score_must_be_none._description: THE KEY ANTI-PATTERN TEST. BHY haircut returned winner_trial=None (no trial cleared the FDR gate). Panel score must be None — the panel must never run on a veto-failed candidate. Regardless of how stellar the candidate's discretionary metrics look, the decision is REJECT_VETO_FAILED. This makes the forbidden anti-pattern (panel outvoting a veto) structurally impossible to express.
bhy_veto_fails_panel_score_must_be_none._invariant: panel_score MUST be None when the BHY veto fails. Any implementation where panel_score is a non-None float on a BHY-failed candidate has broken the one-directional brake.

candidate._note: oos_alpha=99.0 is intentionally huge to confirm the panel cannot use it to outvote the veto

nn1_violation_panel_score_must_be_none._description: NN1 spec-freeze veto fires: validate_nn1_compliance returned is_nn1_honest=False. Panel score must be None regardless of all other metrics. Decision: REJECT_VETO_FAILED.
nn1_violation_panel_score_must_be_none._invariant: panel_score MUST be None when NN1 veto fails.

purge_integrity_veto_fails_panel_score_m._description: Purge/look-ahead integrity veto fires: the per-fold performance series was built on un-purged data. Panel score must be None. Decision: REJECT_VETO_FAILED.
purge_integrity_veto_fails_panel_score_m._invariant: panel_score MUST be None when look-ahead/purge integrity veto fails.

stellar_discretionary_score_cannot_outvo._description: THE FORBIDDEN ANTI-PATTERN IN PURE FORM. BHY veto failed (winner_trial=None). Discretionary metrics are all perfect (1.0). The panel score MUST be None and the decision MUST be REJECT_VETO_FAILED — the discretionary panel has zero ability to resurrect a veto-failed candidate. This test makes the anti-pattern a hard constraint, not a documentation note.
stellar_discretionary_score_cannot_outvo._invariant: panel_score=None AND decision=REJECT_VETO_FAILED regardless of discretionary metric values.

candidate._note: Intentionally maximal discretionary values to confirm they are irrelevant post-veto-failure

stellar_discretionary_score_cannot_outvo._description: Same anti-pattern: NN1 veto failed with a structurally invalid spec. Discretionary scores are all maximal. Panel score MUST be None. Decision: REJECT_VETO_FAILED.

weights_are_named_constants_not_magic_nu._description: The panel's criteria weights must be named constants in the acceptance_gate module — not inline float literals. This fixture documents the requirement; the test is an AST inspection of the implementation.
weights_are_named_constants_not_magic_nu._note: Names must encode the criterion; literals (0.5, 0.5) with no name are a violation.

weights_sum_to_one._description: Panel criteria weights must sum to 1.0 (a valid probability weighting) to ensure the composite panel_score is bounded on a consistent scale. This is a structural invariant, not a producer value.

panel_score_bounded_zero_one._description: panel_score for a veto-passing candidate must be in [0.0, 1.0]. The panel takes weighted averages of per-criterion sub-scores; if sub-scores are in [0,1] and weights sum to 1, the composite is bounded. Bounded output is a structural property the implementer must guarantee.

panel_cannot_adopt_when_candidate_loses_._description: Even when vetoes pass and panel score is non-None, the panel CANNOT adopt a candidate that did NOT beat both OOS baselines. The gate is a one-directional BRAKE — it can only withhold adoption, never promote a non-OOS-superior candidate.
panel_cannot_adopt_when_candidate_loses_._invariant: decision = KEEP_INCUMBENT when oos_alpha <= fallback_oos_alpha OR oos_alpha <= default_oos_alpha, regardless of panel_score value.

candidate._note: oos_alpha < fallback_oos_alpha — candidate lost OOS comparison

panel_can_withhold_adoption_from_oos_sup._description: The panel's legitimate use: a candidate beats OOS baselines (all vetoes pass) but its discretionary panel score is so poor (parameter instability / theory-anchor distance) that the gate withholds adoption. Decision: KEEP_INCUMBENT.
panel_can_withhold_adoption_from_oos_sup._invariant: decision = KEEP_INCUMBENT when panel_score(candidate) < panel_score(incumbent) + PANEL_ADOPT_MARGIN_THRESHOLD even when oos_alpha is strictly superior.

divergence_explainer_must_not_affect_gat._description: The Divergence Explainer is INERT by binding constraint (forbidden-keys doctrine; project_cvar_divergence_validation_wall). It must not appear as a scored criterion in the panel, must not appear in panel_breakdown keys, and its verdict must not influence the gate decision in any form. This test asserts structural exclusion.
divergence_explainer_must_not_affect_gat._invariant: The acceptance_gate module must not import from advisors.divergence_explainer, must not read its verdict, and panel_breakdown must contain no 'divergence_explainer' key.

verdict_struct_schema._description: Every AcceptanceVerdict returned by the gate must contain exactly these keys at a minimum. Shape-only assertion — no producer values pinned.
